verses.text: Mary Magdalene comes early, sees the stone removed, and runs to tell Peter. Empty tomb. The first sign that the Lord is risen.
Mary Magdalene comes early, sees the stone removed, and runs to tell Peter. Empty tomb. The first sign that the Lord is risen.
Peter and John run; the other reaches first and sees the linen. Eager seekers. They hasten to the grave at the news.
Peter and John run; the other reaches first and sees the linen. Eager seekers. They hasten to the grave at the news.
Peter and John run; the other reaches first and sees the linen. Eager seekers. They hasten to the grave at the news.
Peter goes in, sees the linen and folded napkin; 'he saw, and believed.' Order in the tomb. The neat grave-clothes speak of no theft, but resurrection.
Peter goes in, sees the linen and folded napkin; 'he saw, and believed.' Order in the tomb. The neat grave-clothes speak of no theft, but resurrection.
Peter goes in, sees the linen and folded napkin; 'he saw, and believed.' Order in the tomb. The neat grave-clothes speak of no theft, but resurrection.
They knew not yet the Scripture that He must rise; they go home. Slow to understand. Faith dawns before full understanding.
They knew not yet the Scripture that He must rise; they go home. Slow to understand. Faith dawns before full understanding.
Mary stands weeping; the angels ask, 'Why weepest thou?' They have taken my Lord. Grieving love. Her sorrow keeps her at the tomb.
Mary stands weeping; the angels ask, 'Why weepest thou?' They have taken my Lord. Grieving love. Her sorrow keeps her at the tomb.
Mary stands weeping; the angels ask, 'Why weepest thou?' They have taken my Lord. Grieving love. Her sorrow keeps her at the tomb.
She thinks Him the gardener; He says, 'Mary'; she answers, 'Rabboni.' Known by name. The Shepherd calls His own, and she knows His voice.
She thinks Him the gardener; He says, 'Mary'; she answers, 'Rabboni.' Known by name. The Shepherd calls His own, and she knows His voice.
She thinks Him the gardener; He says, 'Mary'; she answers, 'Rabboni.' Known by name. The Shepherd calls His own, and she knows His voice.
Touch Me not, for I ascend; 'go to my brethren'; she tells them. First herald. The mourner becomes the first witness of the risen Lord.
Touch Me not, for I ascend; 'go to my brethren'; she tells them. First herald. The mourner becomes the first witness of the risen Lord.
At evening He stands among them, 'Peace'; He shows His hands and side, and they are glad. Real and risen. The wounds prove it is truly He.
At evening He stands among them, 'Peace'; He shows His hands and side, and they are glad. Real and risen. The wounds prove it is truly He.
'As my Father hath sent me, even so send I you'; He breathes, 'Receive ye the Holy Ghost.' Commissioned. He sends them as the Father sent Him.
'As my Father hath sent me, even so send I you'; He breathes, 'Receive ye the Holy Ghost.' Commissioned. He sends them as the Father sent Him.
'As my Father hath sent me, even so send I you'; He breathes, 'Receive ye the Holy Ghost.' Commissioned. He sends them as the Father sent Him.
Thomas, absent, says, 'Except I shall see... I will not believe.' Doubting. He demands proof before faith.
Thomas, absent, says, 'Except I shall see... I will not believe.' Doubting. He demands proof before faith.
After eight days Jesus comes; 'reach hither thy finger... be not faithless, but believing.' Gentle dealing. He meets the doubter's very terms.
After eight days Jesus comes; 'reach hither thy finger... be not faithless, but believing.' Gentle dealing. He meets the doubter's very terms.
Thomas cries, 'My Lord and my God'; 'blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.' Full confession. The doubter owns Christ as God.
Thomas cries, 'My Lord and my God'; 'blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have believed.' Full confession. The doubter owns Christ as God.
Many signs are unrecorded; 'these are written, that ye might believe... and... have life.' The book's aim. John writes that we may believe and live.
Many signs are unrecorded; 'these are written, that ye might believe... and... have life.' The book's aim. John writes that we may believe and live.
